title: Pteris victoriae
taxonomy: Pteris victoriae is classified under the phylum Pteridophyta, a group of vascular plants commonly known as ferns. It belongs to the family Pteridaceae, specifically within the subfamily Pteridoideae. The genus Pteris is characterized by its unique ferns with multiple fronds and distinct leaf shapes. Pteris victoriae is a species within the genus Pteris, identified by its specific characteristics and distribution. This classification is based on the plant's anatomy, morphology, and genetic analysis.
uses: Pteris victoriae has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. It is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing pain and swelling. The plant is also used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In addition, it is believed to have antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from oxidative stress. Furthermore, Pteris victoriae is used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.

parts: Pteris victoriae is also known as the Indian Brake fern and it is valued for its medicinal properties.
